document.title: IC Partial Test Report
standards: FCC KDB 789033 D02 General UNII Test Procedures New Rules v01r04
FCC KDB 644545 D03 Guidance for IEEE 802 11ac New Rules v01
FCC KDB 662911 D01 Multiple Transmitter Output v02r01
FCC KDB 412172 D01 Determining ERP and EIRP v01r01

sourceSummary.oneLine: IC partial test report CR740701-02AN for Laird Technologies ST60-2230C-P / SU60-2230C-P 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module under RSS-247.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory compliance evidence for radiated emissions and band edge emissions testing against listed RSS, ANSI, and FCC KDB procedures.
keyPoints: The tested sample was evaluated and declared in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.
Summary of Test Results lists radiated emissions against RSS-247 sections 6.2.1.2, 6.2.2.2, 6.2.3.2, and 6.2.4.2 with result Pass.
Testing was received Apr. 07, 2017 and tested Apr. 12 ~ May 10, 2017.
Release record states CR740701-02AN Rev. 01 Initial issue Nov. 29, 2017.

entities.evidence: Brand Name : Laird Technologies Applicant : Laird Technologies

entities.name: International Certification Corp.
entities.evidence: We, International Certification Corp., would like to declare that the tested sample has been evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.

evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: CR740701-02AN Page : 1 of 50 Report Version: Rev. 01

evidence.claim: The document is an IC Partial Test Report.
The equipment is an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module.
The model number listed on the report cover is ST60-2230C-P.
Product details list ST60-2230C-P and SU60-2230C-P models.
evidence.evidenceText: The following models are provided to this EUT. Brand Name Model Name Product Name Description Laird Technologies ST60-2230C-P 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module For marketing purpose SU60-2230C-P

evidence.claim: The report standard on the cover is RSS-247 Issue 2 February 2017.
The tested sample was declared evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.
evidence.evidenceText: We, International Certification Corp., would like to declare that the tested sample has been evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.

evidence.claim: The release record identifies Rev. 01 as the initial issue dated Nov. 29, 2017.
evidence.evidenceText: Release Record Report No. Version Description Issued Date CR740701-02AN Rev. 01 Initial issue Nov. 29, 2017

evidence.claim: The summary test result for radiated emissions is Pass.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of Test Results FCC Rules Test Items Measured Result RSS-247 Section 6.2.1.2 RSS-247 Section 6.2.2.2 RSS-247 Section 6.2.3.2 RSS-247 Section 6.2.4.2 Radiated Emissions [dBuV/m at 3m]: 43.58MHz 38.52 (Margin -1.48dB) - QP Pass

evidence.claim: Testing applied standards include RSS-247, RSS-Gen, ANSI C63.10, ANSI C63.4, and FCC KDB documents.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the specification of EUT, the EUT must comply with following standards and KDB documents. RSS-247 Issue 2 February 2017 RSS-Gen Issue 4 November 2014 ANSI C63.10-2013 ANSI C63.4-2014 FCC KDB 789033 D02 General UNII Test Procedures New Rules v01r04 FCC KDB 644545 D03 Guidance for IEEE 802 11ac New Rules v01 FCC KDB 662911 D01 Multiple Transmitter Output v02r01 FCC KDB 412172 D01 Determining ERP and EIRP v01r01

evidence.claim: The EUT antenna is Laird NanoBlade-IP04 PCB Dipole with IPEX U.FL connector.
evidence.evidenceText: Ant. No. Model Type Connector Operating Frequencies (MHz) / Antenna Gain (dBi) 1 Laird NanoBlade-IP04 PCB Dipole IPEX U.FL

evidence.claim: The EUT power supply type is DC 3.3V from host.
evidence.evidenceText: Power Supply Type DC 3.3V from host

evidence.claim: Radiated emissions testing was performed at test site 03CH03-WS by Aska Huang under ambient condition 22-24°C / 66-67%.
evidence.evidenceText: Test Item Test Site Ambient Condition Tested By Radiated Emissions 03CH03-WS 22-24°C / 66-67% Aska Huang

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is potentially ambiguous: the cover identifies an IC Partial Test Report and IC identifier, while the summary table header says FCC Rules and testing standards include FCC KDB documents.
Detailed measurement plots or numeric tables for pages 16-49 are largely absent from extracted text, limiting verification of individual emission readings.
